title: Current-tyre](https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/playground-series-s6e5/overview%22]},{%22title%22:%22Current-tyre) finish-margin pressure
summary: Model whether the current tyre can plausibly reach the finish by comparing TyreLife plus estimated remaining laps against conservative compound-life windows.
rationale: Current-compound finish pressure was one of the most recurring high-signal feature families in the run and is directly aligned with next-lap pit incentives. Tyres that cannot finish create immediate strategy pressure.
implementation_hint: Assign fixed expected-life constants for SOFT, MEDIUM, HARD, INTERMEDIATE, and WET, then create current-tyre finish margin, shortfall, can-finish flag, normalized pressure, and late-race cannot-finish interactions. Test fixed constants first before any narrow constant tuning.
expected_effect: Small to medium AUC gain with high confidence, especially inside tree models.
risk: Incorrect constants can encode brittle assumptions across seasons and tracks. Do not derive actual stint endpoints from future rows of the same driver-race sequence.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
